programmes.show_description: Brand new series: Janet and Brian's inherited Chinese pottery might be rare... but what are those bubbles in the glaze? Reuben and Lydia race auctioneer Dan for a promising hoard. (Ep6/6)
Gary, 47, has been flown to King's by air ambulance after his motorbike hit a car. Steve, 69, has got a fish bone stuck in his finger while trying to impress a female friend.
A touching episode about people living on their own and reaching out to others. Scaffolder Thomas has been hit on the head by a six-metre pole on a building site.
On the first day in Porto, nurse Shonni from Coventry takes her guests surfing on the Atlantic coast. But with the ocean as calm as a millpond, could her day be a wipeout? (Ep6/20)
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
There are tears, outrage and arguments as the group meet for one last time to find out how much they've been paid and to air their grievances face to face, before the winner is announced.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: In Cumbria, Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit collector Paula, who needs some cash to fix her leaking roof. They restore a Morris Minor and turn unwanted wood into furniture.
Diane and David are relocating to Spain and are looking for a new home near their favourite town of Arboleas in Almería. Can Danni Menzies find their dream character property for £150k?
Essex and Suffolk. The first host, 29-year-old entrepreneur Chris, hopes to impress his guests with a night of French fine dining - and showing off his six pack. (Ep1/5)
Essex and Suffolk. Salon owner Sam opts for a simple, good-looking menu, on a night of bling, laughter and chest waxing. But is it all style and no substance? (Ep2/5)
Essex and Suffolk. Claire lays on a lavish East End feast. But is Sam out for vengeance? And will Joanna's shock proposition to heart-throb Chris take the shine off Claire's night? (Ep3/5)
Essex and Suffolk. Polish party lover Joanna offers her guests a taste of her homeland, with some rather unusual flavour combinations. (Ep4/5)
Essex and Suffolk. Former police officer Mark plans to surprise his guests with some homemade favourites on the final night. And how will Chris respond to Joanna's proposition? (Ep5/5)
The week kicks off at The George Inn in Barton-upon-Humber. Pete's hoping his cosy décor and quality cuisine will give him the edge, but the roadside location isn't up some guests' street.
At Cross Farm Cottages in Holmfirth, Claire reckons she can pip her rivals to the post. But Pete and Alex get antsy about the Artex, while Malaya has a firm opinion about his mattress.
At the New Valron Hotel in Blackpool, Malaya hopes that the seaside setting will blow his rivals out of the water. But a problem with the pipes sends the scores down the tubes...
Phil and Maria hope that their swish sense of style will impress their guests at The Spinney Homestay in Warwickshire. On a pedalo trip, the rivalry revs up with some raucous racing.
The B&Bers meet to settle some scores and find out who's won. Dirty discoveries and sleep issues are on the agenda, and one payment in particular leaves Phil and Maria out in the cold.
Jensen Interceptor: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend head to Southampton to rescue Nick's cherished 1969 Jensen Interceptor.
Rolls-Royce 1929 Doctors Coupe: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend head to Lincolnshire, where the embodiment of a luxurious era awaits, in the form of a 1929 Rolls-Royce Doctors Coupe.
Deaf couple Danni and Salomon try to break through Milo the cavoodle's separation anxiety. And Steve and Fiona hope to match with Bear, an abused Border collie.
Can Leigh and Richard transform a derelict 17th-century Cornish flour mill, which is full of rotten timbers and riddled with structural cracks, into a warm, contemporary four-storey home?
Brand new series - Without Eyes: Italian crime drama from Walter Presents. A blind police intern's highly developed auditory skills make her a gifted sleuth. In Italian/subs.
A 38-year-old has life-threatening injuries after a wall falls on him at work. Seven-year-old Layla-Rose has smashed her front teeth. And a motocross rider has crashed his bike.
Eighty-year-old Ron has fallen out of bed and injured his head. But how badly? And 22-year-old professional carer Evie has been brought into A&E after collapsing at work.
Paramedics treat a woman who's had a seizure and is disorientated, a teenager struggling to breathe, and a woman who's vomited a lot of blood within 48 hours.
A 38-year-old has life-threatening injuries after a wall falls on him at work. Seven-year-old Layla-Rose has smashed her front teeth. And a motocross rider has crashed his bike.
On the second day in Porto, Lou from Doncaster takes the gang off-roading in dirt buggies. It's an all-action, adrenaline-fuelled activity, but not everyone has the stomach for it. (Ep7/20)

Competition kicks off in Lincolnshire, where Kim and David are hosts under fire, for a problem in one of their bathrooms; will a museum quell all of the ire? #WorldPoetryDay
Next up the B&Bers visit Yarm, where extra costs cause substantial alarm. #WorldPoetryDay
To Wales and two hosts' raw basic approach, where dust and parking issues do not charm. At least a brekkie of eggs that are poached, is tasty with nice produce from a farm. #WorldPoetryDay
A cold night is in store in Somerset, where boiler troubles make all the guests fret. #WorldPoetryDay
...then ire and fire are back on payment day; just who will take the winner's plaque away? #WorldPoetryDay
Portugal's beautiful Silver Coast, is the place that Maria and Jess love the most. They hunt for a home, with a fine-tooth comb. But will they be raising a toast? #WorldPoetryDay
Chris and Jane are moving to France, to Lot-et-Garonne given half the chance. With a 200k budget, will they find their dream place? And a brand-new life for them to embrace? #WorldPoetryDay
Clergyman Ken wants a fresh new start; Larnaca in Cyprus has stolen his heart. Five fab properties; how will they fare? And will any be the answer to Ken's prayer? #WorldPoetryDay
The competition takes place in Leeds, where Alice sows her dinner party seeds. Can she win her guests over with a ginormous pavlova, and get the big score she needs? #WorldPoetryDay
Night two in Leeds and Andy's up next; will his posh nosh leave his guests perplexed? Fancy food, a Ferrari and cocktail making; will he get good scores or a rude awakening? #WorldPoetryDay
Third host in Leeds is student Danielle; will her guests be in food heaven or hell? After serving dessert in her wedding dress, one guest's proposal turns into a right mess. #WorldPoetryDay
The fourth host in Leeds is Matt, who seems to impress off the bat. But not everyone's happy, for some it's quite crappy, and dessert ends up going splat! #WorldPoetryDay
Last host in Leeds is jeweller Lynne, who hopes her night sparkles, but can she win? With dinner done, it's karaoke galore; only one can triumph, who has the top score? #WorldPoetryDay
The guests see what's hiding within, Nyoami and Joe's The Trap Inn. Will they like the décor? Could breakfast bring more? And will anyone spot a puffin? #WorldPoetryDay
Damian and also his husband Nigel, welcome their guests to the Newholme Hotel. But what do they think of a surprise in the loo? And a slimy breakfast causes concern too. #WorldPoetryDay
To Portsonachan Hotel & Lodges the B&Bers go, with a keen eye for detail and dust in tow. They visit Inveraray Castle on a spooky trail, but will breakfast be an epic fail? #WorldPoetryDay
There once was a flight attendant called Meg, who decided to run a B&B instead. When her guests came to stay, at her place - Lamlash Bay, did she put the competition to bed? #WorldPoetryDay
Payment day's time to settle some scores, and see what's gone on behind closed doors. After a lavatory let-down and a breakfast sin, whose B&B will take the win? #WorldPoetryDay
Hurt knee, high heart rate; motorcycle accident; 'Your family's here.' #worldpoetryday
A crashed motorbike, a fishbone in a finger, either... help's coming. #WorldPoetryDay
A lonely house stands, unseen behind the windows... time to open up. #WorldPoetryDay
Hurt head and breathless, and not feeling pain always, the ambulance comes. #WorldPoetryDay
